jobs.title: Senior Product Designer
jobs.fitExplanation: This role is a strong fit for Michael Crane. The title 'Senior Product Designer' aligns with his target roles. The industry focus on B2B SaaS, enterprise software, and data/analytics within property and finance sectors directly matches Michael's extensive experience and deep context in complex B2B SaaS products, particularly in data-heavy interfaces and transactional workflows. CoreLogic, being an acquired and publicly traded company, far exceeds the Series C minimum stage requirement. The remote location preference is also met. The job responsibilities, emphasizing leadership in full design lifecycle from 0-to-1 discovery to scaled products, user research, design systems, and cross-functional collaboration, are all areas where Michael excels and seeks to apply his skills. His background in visual systems and craft, which is valued in making complex systems 'delightful and tactile,' is highly relevant to designing intuitive data platforms.
jobs.jobDescription:
CoreLogic is seeking a Senior Product Designer to join our Enterprise Data Solutions team. This role involves leading the design of complex B2B SaaS platforms that provide property data, analytics, and workflow automation for financial institutions and real estate professionals. You will be instrumental in making intricate data systems intuitive and enhancing transactional experiences within our enterprise software. This includes end-to-end design from ambiguous problem spaces (0-to-1) to shipped products, focusing on user research, journey mapping, and developing robust design systems. Collaboration with product management and engineering leads is central, as is a commitment to visual craft and interaction polish to build user trust in high-stakes environments.

Core Responsibilities:
*   Lead the full design lifecycle for enterprise data and workflow products, from discovery to delivery.
*   Conduct user research and synthesize insights to define product strategy and user needs.
*   Design complex data-heavy interfaces, transactional flows, and reporting tools for B2B SaaS.
*   Develop and maintain scalable design systems and component libraries in Figma.
*   Collaborate closely with product managers, engineering, and business stakeholders.
*   Advocate for user-centered design, accessibility, and visual craft within the product.
*   Contribute to shaping design strategy and mentorship within the team.

Key Requirements:
*   10+ years of product design experience, with a strong focus on B2B SaaS or enterprise software.
*   Portfolio demonstrating expertise in complex system design, data visualization, and transactional workflows.
*   Proficiency with Figma for UI design, wireframing, and prototyping.
*   Experience with user research methodologies (e.g., affinity mapping, user flows, journey mapping).
*   Strong understanding of design systems and their implementation at scale.
*   Excellent communication and collaboration skills.
*   Experience in fintech, real estate tech, or related data-intensive industries is a plus.

jobs.title: Senior Product Designer
jobs.fitExplanation: This Senior Product Designer role at Current, a fintech company, is a strong fit for Michael, especially given his experience with payment reconciliation and transactional flows. While Current has a consumer-facing aspect, fintech often involves complex backend systems and B2B partnerships that would leverage Michael's expertise in enterprise software. The remote (US) and contract nature aligns with his open work arrangement, though the contract might deduct slightly from the score if he prefers full-time, the strong industry fit compensates.
jobs.jobDescription:
Current is seeking a Senior Product Designer for a remote (US) contract position, focusing on their fintech platform. This role involves designing thoughtful and high-quality product experiences, particularly around financial transactions, user accounts, and potentially B2B integrations. The designer will work collaboratively with product and engineering teams to enhance existing features and develop new functionalities that simplify complex financial interactions.

Core Responsibilities:
*   Design end-to-end user experiences for a fintech platform, focusing on clarity and usability.
*   Translate complex financial requirements into intuitive and accessible designs.
*   Create wireframes, user flows, and high-fidelity prototypes.
*   Collaborate closely with product managers, engineers, and stakeholders in an agile environment.
*   Participate in user research and feedback sessions to iterate on designs.

Requirements:
*   5+ years of product design experience, with a strong portfolio showcasing fintech or complex transactional system design.
*   Proven ability to deliver high-quality visual and interaction design.
*   Experience working in a fast-paced, collaborative team setting.
*   Proficiency with Figma and other relevant design tools.
*   Strong communication and problem-solving skills.

jobs.title: Lead Product Designer
jobs.fitExplanation: This Lead Product Designer role at Allocate is an excellent match. Allocate operates in Fintech, a sector where Michael has deep context (payment reconciliation, transactional flows). The role is fully remote in the US, aligning perfectly with his preferences. As a Lead designer, it fits his target level, and his experience with complex workflow design, design systems, and driving 0-to-1 initiatives would be highly valued.
jobs.jobDescription:
Allocate is seeking a Lead Product Designer to join their remote team in the US. This role involves owning the end-to-end design of core fintech products, with a focus on creating intuitive and efficient experiences for complex financial workflows. The designer will be responsible for leading product discovery, conducting user research, and developing design solutions from concept to launch. Strong collaboration with product and engineering teams is essential.

Core Responsibilities:
*   Lead the design strategy and execution for fintech product initiatives.
*   Conduct in-depth user research to understand user needs and pain points in financial contexts.
*   Design complex financial workflows, data-heavy interfaces, and transactional experiences.
*   Develop wireframes, interactive prototypes, and high-fidelity UI designs.
*   Collaborate closely with cross-functional teams, including product managers, engineers, and stakeholders.
*   Mentor junior designers and elevate design craft within the team.

Requirements:
*   8+ years of product design experience, with significant experience in fintech or financial services.
*   Proven track record of leading design projects from 0-to-1 through to shipped product.
*   Expertise in complex workflow design and information architecture.
*   Advanced proficiency in Figma and prototyping tools.
*   Strong communication, leadership, and problem-solving skills.

jobs.title: Senior Product Designer
jobs.fitExplanation: This Senior Product Designer role at Humana, a healthtech company, presents a strong fit. While Michael's deep context isn't explicitly in healthtech, his experience in complex B2B SaaS, enterprise software, and simplifying complex workflows is highly transferable. Humana is a large, established company (likely Series C or later). The remote nature is a key match. His focus on clarity and trust in high-stakes problem spaces is relevant in healthcare.
jobs.jobDescription:
Humana is seeking a Senior Product Designer to work remotely, contributing to the design of their healthtech platforms. The role involves leading design initiatives for complex enterprise-level health applications, focusing on user experience, information architecture, and interaction design. The designer will work closely with product and engineering teams to translate user needs into intuitive and accessible digital solutions. Strong emphasis on creating clear and trustworthy experiences in a regulated environment.

Core Responsibilities:
*   Lead end-to-end product design for key features within healthtech platforms.
*   Conduct user research and usability testing to inform design decisions.
*   Develop detailed user flows, wireframes, and prototypes.
*   Collaborate with cross-functional teams including product managers, engineers, and business stakeholders.
*   Ensure designs adhere to accessibility standards and contribute to a consistent design language.

Requirements:
*   8+ years of experience in product design, preferably in enterprise software or complex platforms.
*   Experience designing for regulated industries or high-stakes environments is a plus.
*   Proficiency in modern design tools (e.g., Figma, Sketch, Adobe Creative Suite).
*   Strong communication, presentation, and interpersonal skills.
*   Ability to tackle ambiguous problems and drive design strategy.

jobs.title: Senior Product Designer
jobs.fitExplanation: This role as a Senior Product Designer at GameChanger appears to be a good fit, especially given the remote location and the focus on a software product. While GameChanger's specific industry isn't explicitly B2B SaaS in the snippet, many software companies, particularly those with a user-base requiring complex data or team management, often have B2B elements. Michael's experience in scaling products, simplifying workflows, and cross-functional collaboration would be highly valuable here.
jobs.jobDescription:
GameChanger is seeking a Senior Product Designer to join their remote team. The designer will be responsible for creating intuitive and engaging experiences for their software platform. This role involves the full design lifecycle, from user research and conceptualization to prototyping and contributing to the design system. The ideal candidate will be adept at collaborating with product managers and engineers to deliver impactful product features.

Core Responsibilities:
*   Lead the design of new features and improvements for the core product.
*   Conduct user research to inform design decisions and validate concepts.
*   Develop wireframes, flows, and high-fidelity prototypes.
*   Collaborate closely with product management and engineering teams.
*   Contribute to the evolution and maintenance of the design system.

Requirements:
*   7+ years of experience in product design, with a strong portfolio showcasing user-centered design solutions.
*   Proven ability to work effectively in a remote, cross-functional environment.
*   Proficiency in design and prototyping tools (e.g., Figma, Sketch).
*   Strong understanding of user experience principles and best practices.
*   Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.

jobs.title: Staff UX Product Designer
jobs.fitExplanation: This role aligns well with Michael's fintech adjacent experience (payments, transactional flows) and his expertise in complex B2B SaaS. NinjaTrader operates in the financial services sector, which is a strong fit. The remote nature and Staff level are also ideal. Michael's ability to simplify complex systems and his strong visual craft would be highly valuable in a trading platform environment.
jobs.jobDescription:
NinjaTrader is looking for a Staff UX Product Designer to contribute to their trading platform. The role involves designing intuitive and efficient user experiences for complex financial tools, catering to traders and financial professionals. The designer will be responsible for end-to-end design, from research and ideation to prototyping and implementation support. Collaboration with product management and engineering is key to delivering high-quality, impactful features in a fast-paced fintech environment.

Core Responsibilities:
*   Lead UX design for features within a fintech trading platform.
*   Conduct user research to understand the needs of traders and financial users.
*   Create wireframes, prototypes, and high-fidelity designs for complex financial workflows.
*   Collaborate closely with product managers, engineers, and stakeholders to define and deliver product solutions.
*   Contribute to and maintain a scalable design system for consistency across the platform.

Requirements:
*   8+ years of product design experience, ideally within fintech, financial services, or other complex enterprise software domains.
*   Proven ability to design for data-heavy interfaces and transactional flows.
*   Expertise in user research, information architecture, and interaction design.
*   Proficiency with modern design tools (e.g., Figma).
*   Strong communication and collaboration skills to work with cross-functional teams.

jobs.title: Staff Product Designer
jobs.industry: Cloud Infrastructure, Developer Tools, Enterprise Software, B2B SaaS
jobs.fitExplanation: This role is a strong fit due to its focus on complex enterprise software, cloud infrastructure, and developer tools, which aligns directly with Michael's expertise in B2B SaaS and complex data systems. His experience with platform-level design and cross-functional collaboration is highly relevant. The role being fully remote in the US is also a perfect match for his work arrangement preference. Michael's background in design systems and making complex systems tactile would be highly valued here.
jobs.source: Sequoia Capital, Remote Product Designer Jobs, UI/UX Jobs Board
jobs.jobDescription:
Grafana Labs is seeking a Staff Product Designer to join their incident response management team, focusing on designing user experiences for complex enterprise software. This role involves working within a fully remote R&D organization to create effective user experiences for cloud infrastructure and developer tools. The designer will be responsible for defining solutions that simplify complex workflows and contribute to a robust SaaS platform. Experience with observability and security-related tools is a plus.

Core Responsibilities:
*   Lead design initiatives for incident response management within a remote R&D team.
*   Design effective user experiences for cloud infrastructure and developer tools.
*   Simplify complex workflows in an enterprise SaaS environment.
*   Collaborate with product and engineering teams in a distributed setting.
*   Contribute to the overall design strategy and consistency of the platform.

Requirements:
*   Extensive experience in product design for complex technical software products, developer tools, infrastructure, or B2B/SaaS platforms.
*   Strong portfolio demonstrating impactful work on enterprise-level products.
*   Ability to thrive in a remote, cross-functional R&D environment.
*   Familiarity with observability and computer security concepts.
*   Proficiency in design tools and ability to create high-fidelity prototypes.
